metadata.name: IIA International Standards for the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing
metadata.description: The International Standards for the Professional Practice of Internal Auditing (Standards) are principle-based mandatory requirements consisting of core principles, a definition of internal auditing, a code of ethics, and standards. The 2024 revision represents a comprehensive modernization to address the evolving risk landscape, stakeholder expectations, and technology.
metadata.classification_reasoning: The IIA Standards are the globally recognized mandatory framework for internal audit functions, defining requirements for quality, competency, and conduct of internal audit activities worldwide.
metadata.framework_purpose: To define the nature of internal auditing, establish the basis for measuring internal audit performance, and foster improved organizational processes and operations for all types of organizations globally.
metadata.framework_scope: All internal audit functions regardless of size, industry, or sector. Applicable to chief audit executives, internal auditors, and those providing internal audit services.
framework_objectives: Define the professional practice of internal auditing
Ensure internal audit quality and consistency
Protect the independence and objectivity of internal auditors
Guide governance, risk management, and control assurance
metadata.target_audience: Chief Audit Executives, Internal Auditors, Audit Committees, Boards of Directors, Senior Management, and external parties relying on internal audit work
adoption_approach: Assess current internal audit function against the Standards
Develop Internal Audit Charter aligned with Domain I
Establish quality assurance and improvement program
Obtain external assessment at least every five years
Report conformance to the Standards to governing body

sections: Domain I: Purpose of Internal Auditing
Domain III: Governing the Internal Audit Function
Domain IV: Managing the Internal Audit Function
Domain V: Performing Internal Audit Services
Domain VI: Impact of Internal Auditing

controls.original_reference: Standard 1 - Organizational Objectives, Governance, and Risk
controls.title: Organizational Objectives, Governance, and Risk
controls.description: Internal auditors must have a thorough understanding of the organization's objectives, governance structures, risk management practices, and control environment to provide effective assurance and advisory services.
controls.full_text: Internal auditing adds value by providing stakeholders with objective assessments of whether the organization's governance structures, risk management practices, and internal controls are appropriate and effective. Internal auditors must understand the organization's strategic objectives, risk appetite, and governance framework to prioritize and scope audit work appropriately.
evidence_requirements: Internal Audit Charter defining purpose and scope
Strategic audit plan aligned with organizational objectives

controls.title: Ethical Conduct
controls.description: Internal auditors must adhere to the IIA Code of Ethics, demonstrating integrity, objectivity, confidentiality, and competency in all professional activities.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must comply with the IIA Code of Ethics. The principles of integrity, objectivity, confidentiality, and competency are fundamental to the professional conduct of internal auditors. Auditors must not participate in any activity that impairs their ability to carry out responsibilities ethically.
evidence_requirements: Signed acknowledgment of IIA Code of Ethics

controls.title: Internal Audit Charter
controls.description: The chief audit executive must establish a charter that defines the internal audit function's purpose, authority, and responsibility, formally approved by the board.
controls.full_text: The internal audit charter is a formal document that defines the internal audit activity's purpose, authority, and responsibility. The charter must establish the internal audit activity's position within the organization, authorize access to records, personnel, and physical properties, and define the scope of internal audit activities. The charter must be approved by the board.

controls.title: Independence and Objectivity
controls.description: The internal audit function must be independent, and internal auditors must be objective in performing their work. Independence and objectivity must be free from conditions that threaten unbiased professional judgments.
controls.full_text: The internal audit activity must be independent, and internal auditors must be objective in performing their work. Independence is the freedom from conditions that threaten the ability of the internal audit activity to carry out its responsibilities. Objectivity is an unbiased mental attitude that allows internal auditors to perform engagements in such a manner that they believe in their work product and that no quality compromises are made.

controls.original_reference: Standard 5 - Quality Assurance and Improvement Program
controls.title: Quality Assurance and Improvement Program
controls.description: The chief audit executive must develop and maintain a quality assurance and improvement program (QAIP) that covers all aspects of the internal audit activity.
controls.full_text: The chief audit executive must develop and maintain a quality assurance and improvement program that covers all aspects of the internal audit activity. The program must include both internal and external assessments. Internal assessments must include ongoing monitoring and periodic self-assessments. External assessments must be conducted at least once every five years by a qualified, independent assessor or assessment team from outside the organization.

controls.title: Risk-Based Strategic Audit Planning
controls.description: The chief audit executive must establish a risk-based plan to determine internal audit priorities, consistent with the organization's goals.
controls.full_text: The chief audit executive must establish risk-based plans to determine the priorities of the internal audit activity, consistent with the organization's goals. The plan must be based on documented risk assessment, undertaken at least annually. The CAE must consider input from senior management and the board. The plan must take into account audit engagements or other tasks requested by management and the board.

controls.title: Resource Management
controls.description: The chief audit executive must ensure that internal audit resources are appropriate, sufficient, and effectively deployed to achieve the approved plan.
controls.full_text: The chief audit executive must ensure that internal audit resources are appropriate, sufficient, and effectively deployed to achieve the approved plan. Resources include personnel, technology, and other tools. The CAE must identify resource requirements and obtain sufficient resources from senior management and the board. Resources must be appropriately skilled and experienced.

controls.title: Coordination and Reliance
controls.description: The chief audit executive should share information and coordinate activities with other internal and external providers of assurance to ensure proper coverage and minimize duplication of effort.
controls.full_text: The chief audit executive should share information and coordinate activities with other internal and external providers of assurance and consulting services to ensure proper coverage and minimize duplication of effort. Internal audit may rely on the work of other assurance providers provided it evaluates the providers' objectivity and professional competence.
evidence_requirements: Records of reliance on external work

controls.title: Engagement Planning
controls.description: Internal auditors must develop and document a plan for each engagement including its objectives, scope, timing, resource allocations, and risk assessment.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must develop and document a plan for each engagement. The engagement plan must include objectives, scope, timing, resource allocations, and an assessment of the risk of the engagement. The plan must consider the strategies and objectives of the activity being reviewed and the means by which the activity controls its performance. The plan must be approved by the activity being audited.

controls.title: Engagement Work Program
controls.description: Internal auditors must develop and document work programs that achieve the engagement objectives, reflecting the procedures to be performed during the engagement.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must develop and document work programs that achieve the engagement objectives. The work program must include the procedures for identifying, analyzing, evaluating, and documenting information during the engagement. The work program must be approved before implementation and any adjustments made during the engagement.

controls.title: Engagement Work Performance
controls.description: Internal auditors must identify, analyze, evaluate, and document sufficient information to achieve the engagement objectives.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must identify, analyze, evaluate, and document sufficient information to achieve the engagement's objectives. The information gathered must be sufficient, reliable, relevant, and useful to support engagement conclusions. Internal auditors must evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of controls in responding to risks within the organization's governance, operations, and information systems.

controls.title: Engagement Communication
controls.description: Internal auditors must communicate the engagement results, including objectives, scope, conclusions, recommendations, and action plans.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must communicate the results of engagements. Final communications must include the engagement's objectives, scope, and results. Where applicable, final communications must contain the internal auditors' opinion and must acknowledge satisfactory performance. Communications must be accurate, objective, clear, concise, constructive, complete, and timely.

controls.title: Monitoring Progress
controls.description: The chief audit executive must establish and maintain a system to monitor the disposition of results communicated to management and the board.
controls.full_text: The chief audit executive must establish and maintain a system to monitor the disposition of results communicated to management. The CAE must establish a follow-up process to monitor and ensure that management actions have been effectively implemented or that senior management has accepted the risk of not taking action.

controls.title: Communicating with the Board
controls.description: The chief audit executive must report periodically to the board and senior management on the internal audit activity's purpose, authority, responsibility, and performance.
controls.full_text: The chief audit executive must report periodically to the board and senior management on the internal audit activity's purpose, authority, responsibility, and performance relative to its plan and on its conformance with the Code of Ethics and the Standards. Reporting must also include significant risk exposures and control issues, governance issues, and other matters.
evidence_requirements: Significant risk and control issue communications

controls.original_reference: Standard 15 - Proficiency and Due Professional Care
controls.title: Proficiency and Due Professional Care
controls.description: Engagements must be performed with proficiency and due professional care, reflecting the collective knowledge, skills, and other competencies of the internal audit team.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must possess the knowledge, skills, and other competencies needed to perform their individual responsibilities. The internal audit activity collectively must possess or obtain the knowledge, skills, and other competencies needed to perform its responsibilities. Internal auditors must exercise due professional care by considering scope, complexity, materiality, and adequacy of procedures.

controls.title: Continuing Professional Development
controls.description: Internal auditors must enhance their knowledge, skills, and other competencies through continuing professional development.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must enhance their knowledge, skills, and other competencies through continuing professional development. The CAE must establish policies encouraging ongoing development relevant to the roles and responsibilities of internal audit staff. Development should cover technical audit skills, subject matter expertise, and leadership capabilities.
evidence_requirements: CPE tracking records (minimum 40 hours/year for CIA)

controls.title: Internal Audit Consulting Services
controls.description: When providing consulting services, internal auditors must preserve objectivity and not assume management responsibility, and the nature and scope must be agreed with the engagement client.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors may provide consulting services when the work is consistent with the internal audit activity's overall plan and does not impair independence or objectivity. Consulting engagements must be clearly distinguished from assurance engagements. The nature and scope of consulting engagements must be agreed upon with the engagement client and consistent with the internal audit activity's values, strategy, and objectives.

controls.title: Awareness of Fraud, Cybersecurity, and Technology
controls.description: Internal auditors must have sufficient knowledge to identify indicators of fraud, cybersecurity risks, and technology-related risks, and to evaluate the adequacy of controls.
controls.full_text: Internal auditors must have sufficient knowledge to evaluate the risk of fraud and the manner in which the organization manages that risk, cybersecurity risk management practices, and technology-enabled audit techniques. They must be alert to the possibility of intentional wrongdoing, errors and omissions, inefficiency, waste, ineffectiveness, and conflicts of interest.
evidence_requirements: Data analytics and CAAT tool usage

controls.title: Conformance with the Standards
controls.description: Internal auditors must disclose non-conformance with the Standards and the potential impact when disclosed to stakeholders.
controls.full_text: If the internal audit activity is not in conformance with the Definition of Internal Auditing, the Code of Ethics, or the Standards, the chief audit executive must disclose the non-conformance and the impact of non-conformance to senior management and the board. The disclosure must include the reason for non-conformance and actions being taken to achieve conformance.
evidence_requirements: Use of 'Conforms with IIA Standards' statement documentation

controls.title: Overall Audit Opinions
controls.description: When an overall opinion is issued, it must take into account the expectations of senior management, the board, and other stakeholders and must be supported by sufficient, reliable, and relevant information.
controls.full_text: When the chief audit executive issues an overall opinion about governance, risk management, or internal controls, it must be based on sufficient work performed or directed by the internal audit function. The opinion must be expressed in a manner consistent with the internal audit activity's charter and must be communicated to the board.
